title: Armor Speed
description: Slows you down depending on what armor you're wearing.
body:
Changes players' movement speed depending on the armor they are wearing (and if they have a shield in their offhand).
It scales with the total armor points, and how many pieces of armor you have equipped.

Also, the mod doesn't care if you have a shield in your **main hand**. This is because I think the tradeoff of losing a hotbar slot _and_ having to manually pull it out is balanced.

---

The Fabric version has a custom config thingy because either the documentation for both Cloth Config and YACL is either missing how to set it up in versions past 1.20.x, or I didn't look hard enough. You have to edit it manually in your game's config directory, but at least it refreshes without having to restart or reopen the world; Just do /reload.

---

If you set "mobs" to false in the config, already affected mobs will keep their speed until they die.

The Fabric and NeoForge versions _might_ work on 1.21.9+, but I can't test it currently. You can test it yourself if you want; If it crashes on launch or when loading a world, it's broken.

Also, I removed the source link because I realized far too late that I forgot to actually commit changes to the repository and I don't want it to look like I linked a fake repo.
